text: Headless Claude invocations will use stdin-from-file with explicit per-invocation permission flags: claude -p < prompts/<name>.md --model <model> --permission-mode acceptEdits. Prompt stays versioned on disk; inputs referenced by path inside the prompt file (Claude reads via its Read tool); outputs written by Claudes Write tool; model declared per call so interactive Opus sessions are untouched.
context: User explicitly requested this as an architectural decision. Stdin-from-file keeps prompt reviewable/diff-able; per-invocation --permission-mode means different prompts can have different trust scopes.

text: Create a prompts/ directory (at repo root or under scripts/) to hold reusable headless Claude prompts as versioned .md files.
context: Identified as first implementation step for the headless invocation ADR.

text: Add a thin wrapper script (e.g., scripts/run-headless.sh <prompt-name> [model]) that standardizes the headless invocation pattern; wrapper must cd to repo root before invoking to eliminate CWD ambiguity.
context: Needed to keep invocations consistent across callers and prevent path resolution bugs.

text: Document the headless invocation convention in CONTEXT.md or a new scripts/CLAUDE.md so future agents/contributors know where prompts live and how to add new ones.
context: Identified as part of the headless invocation ADR implementation plan.

text: Decide on a naming/versioning convention for prompt files (e.g., prompts/<domain>/<task>.md) before the directory grows cluttered.
context: Identified proactively as part of the headless prompts/ directory design.

text: Route secrets needed by headless prompts (API keys, tenant IDs) via env vars sourced from etc/.secrets; never inline them in prompt files.
context: Consistent with the existing secrets-never-in-logs invariant in CLAUDE.md.

text: CWD is the silent trap for file references inside prompt files: paths like ./input.txt resolve against the shells CWD at invocation time, not the prompt files location. A prompt working from repo root breaks silently from a subdirectory.
context: Surfaced when discussing file references within the prompts/ convention; fix is wrapper cds to repo root and prompts use REPO_ROOT-relative paths, never bare ./

text: Parameterize headless prompts via env var references initially; graduate to envsubst templates only when prompts start repeating runtime parameters extensively.
context: Three options considered (env vars, concat stdin+CLI args, template engine). Env vars chosen as simplest first step with a clear escalation path.
